description.current: Score ValidationForge primitives: hooks (60% weight), skills (20%), commands (20%). Produces A-F grade, compares against prior benchmarks. Run before releases, after modifications, during audits.
issues: Vague on *which* primitives qualify—all 48 skills or subset? Scope not defined.
Missing timeline guidance: how often to benchmark? When to compare against baseline?
Doesn't clarify if this is for ValidationForge self-audit or user projects

issues.description: The entire skill is 80 lines and references automation ('bash scripts/benchmark/...') without shipping the scripts. Scripts are in the main repo but not bundled with this skill—if a user extracts the skill, they have no way to run it.
Scoring rubric claims 60% weight on Hook Correctness but doesn't explain *what* makes a hook correct—syntax? Functional behavior? Protocol compliance? Vague criteria = unreliable scores.
'Top 10 Skills by Impact' (lines 67-80) is ordered by design importance, not by frequency of use or user pain. Unclear why this ranking matters in a benchmarking skill.
Baseline comparison section assumes prior baseline exists at 'audit-artifacts/benchmark-baseline.json' but never explains how to initialize it on first run.
No explanation of what each script actually validates—does test-hooks.sh run stdin tests or inspect code? Does validate-skills.sh run the skills or just check structure?

Grades table (lines 49-55) is copy-pasted from README with same caveats ('design targets, not calibrated'). This is honest but raises confidence issues—users may not realize the grades are not empirically validated.

strengths: Weight distribution (60/20/20 hooks/skills/commands) is clear and defensible
A-F grading scale is familiar and actionable
Comparison against prior baseline enables regression detection
'Top 10 Skills by Impact' provides a prioritization lens for manual audits

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: All three scripts (test-hooks.sh, validate-skills.sh, aggregate-results.sh) must be bundled or the skill is just guidance without execution capability

top_fixes: Define 'Hook Correctness' scoring rubric in detail—list the exact checks test-hooks.sh performs (exit codes, pattern accuracy, protocol compliance)
Either bundle the benchmark scripts or add clear path guidance (e.g., 'scripts/benchmark/ relative to ValidationForge root')
Explain baseline initialization—what does first run look like? No prior baseline to compare against.
Clarify whether this skill is for VF self-audit or for user projects auditing their own validation setup

test_prompts.prompt: I modified three hooks last week and want to verify I didn't introduce regressions before cutting the next release. Run the benchmark suite and compare against the last baseline.
test_prompts.expected_output: User runs bash scripts/benchmark/test-hooks.sh and gets exit code 0 if all hooks pass functional tests. Runs aggregate-results.sh which outputs new score, compares hook_score against previous baseline in audit-artifacts/benchmark-baseline.json. If regressed, reports which hook(s) failed.

test_prompts.prompt: We're about to add 5 new skills to ValidationForge. What's the minimum bar they need to meet so the overall benchmark grade doesn't drop from A to B?
test_prompts.expected_output: User checks current aggregate score from prior baseline. Calculates: skills weight 20%, current skill_score N%. New skills must have avg frontmatter/description score ≥ X to keep aggregate ≥ 90. (Actual math depends on current baseline.)

test_prompts.prompt: I want to understand exactly why command validation failed. What does validate-cmds.sh check for?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill should explain that validate-cmds.sh checks each command's SKILL.md for required frontmatter (name, description, triggers, etc.), description length, and cross-reference validity. Currently it just says 'command frontmatter validation'.
